summary: Comprehensive and well-structured contract covering all three Sprint 2 features (F5: Player Movement, F6: Player Combat, F7: Player Animation & Visuals). 28 criteria with clear pass/fail thresholds, appropriate test methods (playwright for observable behavior, code-review for internal architecture), and full coverage of the product spec's requirements. Regression sprint [1] correctly listed. Accepted with minor advisory notes below.

F5_PlayerMovement.coverage: Complete. All 8 movement mechanics from the product spec are covered: run (1.6x), variable-height jump, wall slide, wall jump, coyote time (6 frames), jump buffering (8 frames), crouch, crouch-slide.

F6_PlayerCombat.coverage: Complete. Melee attack, charge attack (break blocks), jump attack (downward strike + bounce), 3-hit health, 5-life system, and invincibility frames are all covered. Correctly notes that combat is tested against breakable blocks and hazards since enemies don't exist until Sprint 5.

F7_PlayerAnimationVisuals.coverage: Complete. All animation states from the spec are covered: idle (breathing), walk (4 frames), run (6 frames), jump/fall distinction, wall slide, crouch, attack, hurt, death. Particle effects (landing dust, run dust, wall slide sparks) and state machine architecture are included.

Integration.coverage: C-27 validates all input bindings work. C-28 is an explicit regression check over Sprint 1 functionality. Combined with regressionSprints: [1], this provides double-coverage on regression.

testMethodDistribution.assessment: Good balance. Visual/behavioral criteria use playwright; internal architecture (animation frame counts, state machine structure, particle lifecycle) use code-review. All code-review criteria have specific verifiable checkpoints.

criteriaIssues.issue: Test step 1 says 'Position the player next to a tall wall in the air' without specifying how. The evaluator needs to jump the player near a wall. The test level from Sprint 1 must include a suitable tall wall section, or the notes should specify the level will be extended with one.
criteriaIssues.resolution: Notes already state the test level should be extended for wall jump areas. Acceptable as-is; the evaluator can navigate to a wall and jump.

criteriaIssues.issue: Coyote time testing is timing-sensitive. Playwright can control keyboard timing precisely, but the 100ms window is tight. The negative test (300ms later = no jump) is good for verification. However, the test relies on the evaluator knowing the exact frame rate and being able to measure 'leaving the platform' precisely.
criteriaIssues.resolution: Acceptable. Playwright's keyboard.down/up with delays can hit 100ms windows. The evaluator can also check via debug API or code review as fallback.

criteriaIssues.issue: Step 4 says 'Verify the jump buffer window is approximately 100-150ms' but this is extremely hard to measure precisely via playwright. The observable test (press jump before landing, auto-jump occurs) is sufficient.
criteriaIssues.resolution: The threshold correctly focuses on the observable behavior ('Pressing jump slightly before landing causes an automatic jump upon touchdown'). The evaluator should focus on this, not the exact ms window.

criteriaIssues.issue: Code-review is appropriate since no enemies exist, but the threshold says 'hitting something bounces the player upward' without specifying what to hit. Since breakable blocks exist (C-10), the jump attack could be tested against them if the code supports it.
criteriaIssues.resolution: Code-review is the right call. The evaluator verifies the code path exists and the bounce impulse is applied on hit detection. No runtime test needed for Sprint 2.

criteriaIssues.issue: Overlap: C-12 step 4 tests invincibility frames, and C-14 is entirely about invincibility frames. This is acceptable redundancy — C-12 tests the health system end-to-end while C-14 isolates the visual/functional invincibility behavior.
criteriaIssues.resolution: No change needed. Distinct focus areas despite partial overlap.

criteriaIssues.issue: C-28 is an explicit regression criterion, and regressionSprints: [1] also triggers full Sprint 1 regression. This provides double-coverage. The explicit C-28 is useful as a quick sanity check even if the full regression suite also runs.
criteriaIssues.resolution: No change needed. Belt-and-suspenders approach is fine for regression.

coverageGaps.description: No explicit criterion for sprite flipping on direction change during Sprint 2 states (wall slide reversal on wall jump, attack facing direction).
coverageGaps.assessment: Sprint 1 C-28 (regression) covers basic sprite flipping. C-04 step 5 checks facing direction change after wall jump. C-09 checks attack 'in the direction the player faces.' Implicitly covered.

coverageGaps.description: No criterion verifying the design spec's player colors (#3A7BD5 blue body, #E8DCC8 face/hands) are maintained in the new animation system.
coverageGaps.assessment: Sprint 1 C-27 already validated these colors. Regression should catch any deviation. Not a gap worth blocking on.

coverageGaps.description: No criterion for the design spec's attack animation timing (3 frames / 0.15s for swing, 4 frames / 0.2s for charge release).
coverageGaps.assessment: C-09 and C-10 test the observable attack behavior. Exact frame timing can be validated in code-review during evaluation. Not critical to contract.

outOfScopeAssessment: Well-defined. Correctly defers game state machine, HUD, world map, save/load, enemies, audio, screen shake, and advanced VFX to later sprints. The note about using breakable blocks and hazards as combat test proxies is pragmatic and correct.
regressionAssessment: regressionSprints: [1] is correct. Sprint 2 builds directly on Sprint 1's physics, tiles, camera, and rendering. All 28 Sprint 1 criteria should be re-verified. C-28 provides an additional explicit regression check within this sprint's own criteria.
complexityAssessment: 28 criteria is high for a single sprint, but Sprint 2 genuinely covers three major features with deep interaction between movement, combat, and animation systems. The product spec's Sprint 2 deliverable is described as 'High' complexity. The criterion count is proportional to scope.
notes: The contract is well-aligned with the product spec's F5/F6/F7 definitions. Every sub-feature mentioned in the spec has a corresponding criterion.

Test methods are appropriately chosen: playwright for observable behavior, code-review for internal architecture that can't be easily observed (frame counts, state machine structure, particle lifecycle).

The note about extending the test level is important — the generator must ensure the level has tall walls, low ceilings, and breakable blocks for testing wall mechanics, crouching, and charge attacks.

Timing-sensitive tests (C-05 coyote time, C-06 jump buffer) may be challenging to automate perfectly. The evaluator should use reasonable tolerance and fall back to code-review if precise timing proves unreliable in Playwright.
